full_prompt: Design the primary base chassis plate of an collegiate robotics benchmark 'Kung Fu Quest' R1 robot. Per the 2026 rulebook, R1 must fit a 1000 x 1000 x 1000 mm starting envelope, with extension allowed up to 1000 x 1800 x 1300 mm during play; pair transport envelope is 700 x 700 x 700 mm and pair total mass including R2 <= 57 kg, implying an R1 mass target of around 30 kg with a 150 mm-high lifting arm tower mounted on the chassis. The chassis base plate is the rigid plate to which four omni-wheel drive modules, one tower post, battery, and tool payload attach. Geometry: rectangular plate 950 mm (x) x 950 mm (y) x 6 mm (z) machined from 6061-T6 aluminium, with 4 corner wheel-module bolt patterns (each 100 mm x 100 mm, 4 x M6 through-holes), a central 150 mm diameter circular cutout for a tower-post feedthrough surrounded by a 200 mm OD ring of 8 x M8 bolt holes on a 180 mm bolt circle, a battery bay cutout of 400 mm x 200 mm centered 250 mm from the rear edge, and four electronics-mount pads on an 800 mm x 800 mm square pattern each with 4 x M4 threaded holes. Material: 6061-T6 (Young's 69 GPa, Poisson 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa). Load cases: LC1 (Static weight) — 30 kg total robot mass (electronics, wheels, tower, battery, tool payload of 10 kg) rest on the plate; modeled as 100 N at each wheel-module bolt pattern pushing up (reaction), 200 N at the tower-post ring pressing down (tower weight), and 100 N at the battery bay as distributed load; LC2 (Maximum tool arm extended) — tower arm fully extended forward; vertical 150 N downward plus 100 Nm moment about the tower-ring axis (representative of arm at 1 m reach with 10 kg tool); LC3 (Collision with opponent) — 20 g lateral shock in the worst orientation with all four wheel-module bolt patterns fixed; LC4 (Transport drop) — 0.8 m drop test with robot lying on its base plate; modeled as 15 g vertical shock with plate supported on edges; LC5 (Handle lift) — two edge handles at the plate's long edges each take 300 N vertical lift during manual handling, with wheel patterns unconstrained. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under LC1, LC2, LC3, LC4, LC5 < 184 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) max plate deflection under LC1 + LC2 superposed < 3 mm (to keep tower-post perpendicular for accurate tool placement); (R3) first-mode linear buckling under LC3 lateral (plate + mounted tower buckling) >= 2.0; (R4) plate mass < 5 kg so R1 stays under its 30 kg budget; (R5) under LC4, no permanent deflection > 1 mm at handles after event (linear-static elastic check surrogate); (R6) outer envelope under LC2 must not exceed 1000 x 1800 x 1300 mm. Primary verification: linear static for LC1, LC2, LC3, LC5; quasi-static with 15 g factor for LC4; linear buckling for LC3. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Output one STEP AP242 single-body plate plus a report listing plate mass, max von Mises per case, max combined deflection LC1+LC2, buckling factor under LC3.
short_prompt:
Design the primary base chassis plate of a collegiate robotics benchmark 'Kung Fu Quest' R1 robot. R1 start envelope 1000 x 1000 x 1000 mm (extension allowed to 1000 x 1800 x 1300 mm during play), pair transport 700 x 700 x 700 mm, pair mass (R1 + R2) <= 57 kg with R1 target ~30 kg.

Envelope: 950 x 950 x 6 mm 6061-T6 plate with four 100 x 100 mm 4xM6 corner wheel-module patterns, a 150 mm central tower feedthrough surrounded by a 200 mm ring of 8 x M8 on a 180 mm bolt circle, a 400 x 200 mm battery-bay cutout 250 mm from the rear edge, and four 4xM4 electronics-mount pads on an 800 x 800 mm square pattern.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5; tower-perpendicular deflection under combined LC1+LC2 < 3 mm; linear buckling eigenvalue under lateral collision >= 2.0; handle-lift residual < 1 mm.

Load cases.
- Static weight: 100 N up per wheel-module pattern, 200 N down at the tower ring, 100 N battery-bay UDL.
- Tool arm fully extended: 150 N down + 100 N-m moment about the tower axis.
- Opponent collision: 20 g lateral with all four wheel patterns fixed.
- Transport drop: 0.8 m drop on the base = 15 g vertical with plate edge-supported.
- Handle lift: 300 N vertical at each of two edge handles with wheel patterns free.

prompt.title: collegiate robotics benchmark R1 chassis base plate
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 950 x 950 x 6 mm chassis base plate for an R1 robot in the 2026 'Kung Fu Quest' rulebook, 30 kg robot mass, 10 kg tool payload, and tower-post central cutout.

eval_coverage: drop test approximated as static shock
